item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even with all our studies, our information, our hierarchy and chain of command, our rules and regulations… we are but children, and we know not what we do.

I love to see skips that share a critical review of Foundation activities, that "Grown up that balks at what they did in their youth" feels is so relatable it hurts, and big entities end up resembling people in one way or another. Upvoted with all my might.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems to be a date error under Document 2750-4, but I'm not confident enough to change it. Excellent article otherwise of course, always love a historical skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Foundation did a bad with some mythological creature in the past" is a weirdly beloved micro-trope of this site, and one which always fails to interest me. This is competently executed, but I just don't care very much.

I will say that "yeah, we kinda screwed up, but no, we're not going to change anything about your current treatment" is a nice commentary, but it's still a -1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it naaldloshii or naadloshii? The initial letter from the ASCI agent says naaldloshii while the continuation below it says naadloshii.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit by hedabla99 was reverted, as it changed the meaning of the original content. Per the Site Rules, stylistic edits require permission from the original author or staff before they can be made.

item.title: Provenance
item.content:encoded:
Top photo is Nayenezgani, a Navajo, photographed in 1904 by Edward S. Curtis. Photo is public domain.

"authentic Navajo Ceremonial Costume (…) dark leather mask, fur ruff, cloth girdle, silver concho belt and necklaces."

Second photo is Tobadzishini, also a Navajo in ceremonial costume, also photographed in 1904 by Edward S. Curtis, also public domain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It occurs to me that I wish there was a "proto-foundation" tag for these SCPs that tie into the pre-history of the organization. I love 'em!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me Aphocalypto by Mel Gibson so much. I hate Gibson but I loved Aphocalypto.
Something in gray gradient of ethics like this SCP always makes me feel an immoral pleasure. Chills up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some anvils have to be dropped. Well, kind of, it really was not anvilicious, but the parallels are good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Admittedly, I wanted to make an SCP based on skinwalkers in a different light. I would initially denounce this article as the standard fare on mythology-based SCPs, but the last portion is somewhat heart-wrenching and it shows why the Foundation is as it is.

My upvote is based on that account, that an organisation like the Foundation has to restrain themselves from excessive behaviour.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Is there a way to delete all my comments?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those pictures are excellent.

(So is your article, I just wanted to note how great they are.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hopefully you remember what I said the first time round and can decide how much my criticisms then might still apply, because I don't. I prefer it to the previous version, but I don't know whether or not I actually like the article. I'm no-voting until I can work out what my opinion on this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an excellent example of the foundation being cold, not cruel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that they're "just skinwalkers."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Couple grammar things — "though this is mostly for mostly historical purposes" — one of those mostlys doesn't need to be there. Also, you spell naaldloshii at least two different ways.

My upvote, you can have it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So much upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's quite good. The pictures and the quality of the pictures adds to the eeriness of the creatures, but the way they are being mercilessly hunted and exterminated reminds me of the European efforts to eradicate the Native Americans and made me sympathize with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with the "it's just skinwalkers" complaint, but the dilemma faced by the Foundation between containing an anomaly and destroying a culture is nice, and the SCP was overall very well done. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it! Keep it up! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Improved from the last version. +1.

item.title: Author Overview
item.content:encoded:
And this one's back!

This is a skip that I posted quite a while ago (roughly 7 months or so) that I self-deleted to rewrite. It's back now, and hopefully better than the original version. Thanks to Faminepulse for the retouched images, and thanks to kaktus and Dr Leonard for taking a look at the new version.

I tried to address the "It's just skinwalkers" criticism from the original, and I also edited the impact of the article to make it more focused. Hope you enjoy.
